100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 27, 1926, the world was abuzz with significant developments. In England, a strike by railway workers threatened to disrupt transportation across the country, as tensions between labor and government escalated. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing effects of Prohibition continued to shape public life, with debates over its future heating up. In Germany, political instability persisted as the Weimar Republic faced mounting challenges from both the left and right.
Updated: Monday, April 27, 2026 at 08:09:35 AM CDT
